main_dirt.bias_notes: Clockwise. A classic outback Queensland dirt circuit. The surface is completely composed of dirt and sand, which creates significant, heavy kickback during a race. The flat layout and relatively short 300m home straight heavily favor horses that can jump cleanly, dictate the pace, and stay out of the dust cloud. Deep closers are at a massive mathematical and physical disadvantage.
main_dirt.post_bias: Inside barriers (1-4) are highly advantageous. Securing the rail and the lead (or sitting 'outside-leader') is critical to avoid the blinding dirt kickback. Wide draws force horses to either burn maximum energy to cross over or drop to the rear and eat dirt.

handicapping_angles.outback_dirt_specialists: Turf ratings do not translate here. Heavily upgrade horses with proven form on the Central West dirt circuit (e.g., Barcaldine, Longreach, Roma). Horses stepping onto the dirt for the first time—regardless of their grass speed figures—are massive risks.
handicapping_angles.kickback_survival: Aggressively upgrade pure front-runners ('E' types) and downgrade 'Swoopers'. If a horse is known to be a timid stalker or slow out of the gates, the heavy outback kickback will frequently cause them to resent the race and drop the bit entirely.
handicapping_angles.the_coastal_class_drop: Horses occasionally ship inland from coastal provincial turf tracks like Rockhampton or Mackay. While they possess a massive class edge on paper, treat them with caution unless their running style shows elite early gate speed to stay out of the dirt.
